title: Homogenization of the Peierls-Nabarro model for dislocation dynamics
summary:
This paper is concerned with a result of homogenization of an
integro-differential equation describing dislocation dynamics. Our model
involves both an anisotropic L\'{e}vy operator of order 1 and a potential
depending periodically on $u/\ep$. The limit equation is a non-local
Hamilton-Jacobi equation, which is an effective plastic law for densities of
dislocations moving in a single slip plane.

title: Stability of ZND detonations for Majda's model
comment:
This is the same as the paper published in Quart. Appl. Math. 70
  (2012), with minor fix. We allow u_+ \geq 0(not only u_+=0) that was
  mistakenly omitted in original treatment. The analogy of the model for gas
  dynamics is only for u_+ \geq 0, the "physical" range defined by Majda
summary:
We evaluate by direct calculation the Lopatinski determinant for ZND
detonations in Majda's model for reacting flow, and show that on the nonstable
(nonnegative real part) complex half-plane it has a single zero at the origin
of multiplicity one, implying stability. Together with results of Zumbrun on
the inviscid limit, this recovers the result of RoqueJoffre-Vila that viscous
detonations of Majda's model also are stable for sufficiently small viscosity,
for any fixed detonation strength, heat release, and rate of reaction.

title: Topological Properties of Strong Solutions for the 3D Navier-Stokes   Equations
summary:
In this note we give a criterion for the existence of global strong solutions
for the 3D Navier-Stokes system for any regular initial data.

title: Radial Symmetry of Large Solutions of Semilinear Elliptic Equations with   Convection
summary:
We study radial symmetry of large solutions of the semi-linear elliptic
problem \Delta u + \nabla h.\nabla u = f(|x|,u), and we provide sharp
conditions under which the problem has a radial solution. The result is
independent of the rate of growth of the solution at infinity.

title: Derivation of Orowan's law from the Peierls-Nabarro model
comment: 23 pages. arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:1007.2915
summary:
In this paper we consider the time dependent Peierls-Nabarro model in
dimension one. This model is a semi-linear integro-differential equation
associated to the half Laplacian. This model describes the evolution of phase
transitions associated to dislocations. At large scale with well separated
dislocations, we show that the dislocations move at a velocity proportional to
the effective stress. This implies Orowan's law which claims that the plastic
strain velocity is proportional to the product of the density of dislocations
by the effective stress.

title: Global existence of solutions to a parabolic-elliptic chemotaxis system   with critical degenerate di ffusion
summary:
This paper is devoted to the analysis of non-negative solutions for a
degenerate parabolic-elliptic Patlak-Keller-Segel system with critical
nonlinear diffusion in a bounded domain with homogeneous Neumann boundary
conditions. Our aim is to prove the existence of a global weak solution under a
smallness condition on the mass of the initial data, there by completing
previous results on nite blow-up for large masses. Under some higher regularity
condition on solutions, the uniqueness of solutions is proved by using a
classical duality technique.
